definitions.technical: The remaining time until a financial instrument matures or becomes due. Reserve asset tenor affects liquidity because longer-tenor instruments cannot be converted to cash at par until maturity without potential market loss.
definitions.lay: The amount of time left before a financial instrument pays out its full value. A Treasury bill with a 90-day tenor will return its face value in 90 days. Shorter tenors generally mean the asset can be turned into cash sooner.
definitions.parenthetical: (the remaining time until a financial instrument matures, affecting its liquidity characteristics)
definitions.legal_significance: Reserve asset tenor is relevant to the operational capability to monetize under proposed Section 15.11(a)(2). Longer-tenor instruments may comply with composition requirements but reduce practical liquidity, especially during stress periods when early sale would incur market losses.
